title: Decision Memorandum
source_text:
5. Decision Memorandum. When only requesting an approval/disapproval decision from a single addressee, it is appropriate to type a decision block at the left margin, two lines below the signature line in the following format:
COMMANDING OFFICER DECISION:
_____________________ Approved
_____________________ Disapproved
_____________________ Other
plain_language_rule: When only requesting an approval/disapproval decision from a single addressee, type a decision block at the left margin, two lines below the signature line in the following format: COMMANDING OFFICER DECISION: followed by three underlined options: Approved, Disapproved, and Other. The decision block format uses consistent underlining and spacing.
renderer_impact: When requesting only an approval/disapproval decision from a single addressee, render a decision block at the left margin two lines below the signature line. Include COMMANDING OFFICER DECISION: heading. Render three underlined options: Approved, Disapproved, and Other.
notes: Source text resolved from manual section 10-2.5. This rule governs the decision memorandum format for requesting approval/disapproval from a single addressee. The decision block is typed at the left margin, two lines below the signature line. Includes COMMANDING OFFICER DECISION: heading and three underlined options: Approved, Disapproved, and Other.
